name: Check Every Other Interval
summary: For each interval, scan all the others to see if any single interval covers it; count the intervals nothing covers.
visualizationIntent: init: show the intervals (original order) as cells and count 0. outer-loop: pointer i marks the interval being tested; assume not covered. inner-loop: pointer j scans each other interval as a candidate cover (compared/blue against i/current). check: if candidate j starts at or before i and ends at or after i, mark interval i covered (dimmed) and stop scanning. keep: if no interval covered i, count grows. return: freeze on the final survivor count.

lineExplanations.3: Start the survivor counter at zero.
lineExplanations.4: Store the number of intervals for both loops.
lineExplanations.5: Pick each interval i to test for being covered.
lineExplanations.6: Assume interval i is not covered before scanning.
lineExplanations.9: Scan every other interval j as a possible cover.
lineExplanations.10: Skip comparing interval i with itself.
lineExplanations.11: Move to the next candidate when j equals i.
lineExplanations.14: Check whether j starts at or before i and ends at or after i, which means j covers i.
lineExplanations.16: Stop scanning once a covering interval is found.
lineExplanations.17: If nothing covered interval i, keep it.
lineExplanations.18: Count interval i as a survivor.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: defines the class wrapper the platform expects.
syntaxExplanations.2: def removeCoveredIntervals(self, intervals): defines the method; intervals is a list of [start, end] pairs.
syntaxExplanations.3: count = 0 creates an integer counter starting at zero.
syntaxExplanations.4: n = len(intervals) stores how many intervals there are.
syntaxExplanations.5: for i in range(n): loops i over each interval to test.
syntaxExplanations.6: covered = False assumes interval i is not covered until proven otherwise.
syntaxExplanations.7: a = intervals[i][0] reads interval i's start.
syntaxExplanations.8: b = intervals[i][1] reads interval i's end.
syntaxExplanations.9: for j in range(n): loops j over every possible covering interval.
syntaxExplanations.10: if i == j: detects the interval compared with itself.
syntaxExplanations.11: continue skips to the next j without comparing.
syntaxExplanations.12: c = intervals[j][0] reads candidate interval j's start.
syntaxExplanations.13: d = intervals[j][1] reads candidate interval j's end.
syntaxExplanations.14: if c <= a and b <= d: is true exactly when interval j covers interval i.
syntaxExplanations.15: covered = True marks interval i as covered.
syntaxExplanations.16: break stops the inner scan early.
syntaxExplanations.17: if not covered: runs when no interval covered interval i.
syntaxExplanations.18: count += 1 counts interval i as a survivor.
syntaxExplanations.19: return count sends back the number of surviving intervals.
